jokes.text: You jerks are just all mad 'cause I'm gonna be the one to shatter Boss Hogg's drinking record.
jokes.setup: Dee claims she'll break a drinking record
jokes.punchline: She confuses Boss Hogg (fictional TV character) with Wade Boggs (real baseball player)
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Dee's ignorance and overconfidence. Sets up the running gag about Wade Boggs being alive/dead.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You jerks are just all mad 'cause I'm gonna be the one to shatter Boss Hogg's drinking record.

jokes.text: Dee, Boss Hogg was a big, fat redneck from The Dukes of Hazzard. Wade Boggs is a Hall of Fame third baseman.
jokes.setup: Dee's confusion between fictional and real person
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact correction highlighting how wrong she is
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response that clarifies the absurdity of Dee's mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dee, Boss Hogg was a big, fat redneck from The Dukes of Hazzard. Wade Boggs is a Hall of Fame third baseman.

jokes.text: That's why we're doing this, to honor his memory, okay? May he rest in peace.
jokes.setup: Mac treating this as a solemn tribute
jokes.punchline: He thinks Wade Boggs is dead when he's very much alive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's false reverence and ignorance.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's why we're doing this, to honor his memory, okay? May he rest in peace.

jokes.text: First off, Wade Boggs is very much alive.
jokes.setup: Mac's memorial speech
jokes.punchline: Charlie's flat correction of basic fact
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered deadpan correction that became a meme. Charlie's matter-of-fact delivery is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: First off, Wade Boggs is very much alive.

jokes.text: Which, to be honest, is an absolutely insane amount of beer. Nobody can drink that much.
jokes.setup: Charlie noting the impossibility of drinking 70 beers
jokes.punchline: Sets up the absurdity they're about to attempt
jokes.explanation: Functional setup for the episode's premise, but not particularly funny on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Which, to be honest, is an absolutely insane amount of beer. Nobody can drink that much.

jokes.text: Not with an attitude like that.
jokes.setup: Charlie's reasonable observation about impossibility
jokes.punchline: Mac treats drinking 70 beers as a matter of positive thinking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's delusional confidence and toxic positivity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Not with an attitude like that.

jokes.text: That's why we're doing this, all right? To honor his memory. Rest in peace, Wade. Again, he is still alive.
jokes.setup: Mac repeating the memorial bit
jokes.punchline: Charlie's exasperated repeat correction
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that establishes the running gag. Charlie's delivery gets funnier with repetition.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's why we're doing this, all right? To honor his memory. Rest in peace, Wade.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Again, he is still alive.

jokes.text: Always sticking me with the nerds.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about her seating assignment
jokes.punchline: She's calling other people nerds while attempting to break a beer drinking record on a plane
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Dee's lack of self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Always sticking me with the nerds.

jokes.text: We're doing a Wade Boggs-type thing, and that makes me sort of the Commissioner Bud Selig of the group.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining their drinking contest to flight attendant
jokes.punchline: He's appointed himself baseball commissioner of a beer drinking contest
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Mac's need for authority and his overthinking of simple situations.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We're doing a Wade Boggs-type thing, and that makes me sort of the Commissioner Bud Selig of the group.

jokes.text: It's... really cold. Man, Dee... You really have to slow down a little bit.
jokes.setup: Dee chugging beer aggressively
jokes.punchline: Mac's weak response to her alarming drinking pace
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy moment, mostly setup for later escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's... really cold. Man, Dee... You really have to slow down a little bit.

jokes.text: how many beers are we allowed to order at once? I'm not sure. No one has ever asked that before.
jokes.setup: Frank asking about beer ordering limits
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant has never encountered this problem before
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment highlighting how unusual their behavior is.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: how many beers are we allowed to order at once?

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: I'm not sure. No one has ever asked that before.

jokes.text: How about you make it an even dozen? How about I start you with two apiece and then we just take it from there?
jokes.setup: Frank asking for six beers
jokes.punchline: He immediately escalates to twelve, flight attendant tries to manage the situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Frank's lack of restraint and the attendant's reasonable response.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: How about you make it an even dozen?

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: How about I start you with two apiece and then we just take it from there?

jokes.text: I'll be taking care of everybody on the Boggs account.

jokes.punchline: He treats their drinking contest like a business account
jokes.explanation: Great Frank moment treating absurd situation with business-like seriousness.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'll be taking care of everybody on the Boggs account.

jokes.text: Oh, and by the way, do you sell condoms? No, I'm afraid we don't. Aw... Yeah. I'll have to figure something else out.

jokes.punchline: His disappointment and casual mention of backup plans
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Frank's inappropriate behavior and casual attitude toward it.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh, and by the way, do you sell condoms?

dialogue.speaker: Flight Attendant
dialogue.line: No, I'm afraid we don't.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Aw... Yeah. I'll have to figure something else out.

jokes.text: I'm gonna become the first-ever member of the Air Sex Society.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his secondary goal
jokes.punchline: He's invented a fake prestigious-sounding organization
jokes.explanation: Good Frank absurdity, making up grandiose names for crude behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm gonna become the first-ever member of the Air Sex Society.

jokes.text: Because that's already a thing. It's called the Mile High Club. People have been doing it since planes have been around. That name sucks.

jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's ego and Dennis as voice of reason.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Because that's already a thing. It's called the Mile High Club. People have been doing it since planes have been around.

dialogue.line: That name sucks.

jokes.text: I will make you a bet that I plow somebody on this fight, somewhere other than the bathroom, before we hit the Great Lakes.
jokes.setup: Frank's sex goal
jokes.punchline: He adds increasingly specific conditions to make it more challenging
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Frank's absurd goals, adding arbitrary constraints.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I will make you a bet that I plow somebody on this fight, somewhere other than the bathroom, before we hit the Great Lakes.

jokes.explanation: Great Mac character moment showing his over-seriousness about trivial things.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There's no betting, okay? That compromises the integrity of the game.

jokes.text: not because you told me to, but because I'm commissioner and that's my job.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: not because you told me to, but because I'm commissioner and that's my job.

jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment showing the contest is getting out of hand.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: 29... beers.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: The man ate a chicken before every game, all right? That's why they called him the Chicken Man.

dialogue.line: Mama needs another pop, pop Hey, pop, soda pop, soda pop, soda pop, soda pop, nah...

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Greenies. Uppers. Vtt! Feel like I got a rocket up my ass.

jokes.text: As the Bud Selig of the group, it's my job to quietly sweep this under the rug before anybody finds out.

dialogue.speaker: Mac

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Don't worry about it. I laced it with sleeping pills. He's gonna be out like a light in two minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis

jokes.text: Boggsy didn't drink six gallons of beer because he was obsessed with, like, breaking some record. You know what I mean? He did it 'cause he was just, like, thirsty, you know? And looking to pass the time.

dialogue.line: Boggsy didn't drink six gallons of beer because he was obsessed with, like, breaking some record. You know what I mean? He did it 'cause he was just, like, thirsty, you know? And looking to pass the time.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Who-who cares about 27 beers? We're not selling anything. Like, I think you're taking the commissioner thing way... Look, you lost the chugging contest, man.

jokes.text: So any of you dicknips think you can slug it down faster than me, you're welcome to get your fat asses up here to try.

dialogue.speaker: Pilot
dialogue.line: So any of you dicknips think you can slug it down faster than me, you're welcome to get your fat asses up here to try.

jokes.text: Attention, passengers. This is your captain, Boss Hogg, speaking. And this cold slice of heaven is my 40th beer of the afternoon. So any of you dicknips think you can slug it down faster than me, you're welcome to get your fat asses up here to try.
jokes.setup: Pilot making announcement
jokes.punchline: He's drunk, challenging passengers, and using Boss Hogg name

jokes.text: I got a hundred extra beers in a checked bag, okay? Now, according to movies like Executive Decision and Passenger 57, there is a secret hatch on every plane that allows me to travel freely throughout the aircraft.

jokes.explanation: Excellent Mac moment showing his movie-based understanding of reality.

dialogue.line: I got a hundred extra beers in a checked bag, okay? Now, according to movies like Executive Decision and Passenger 57, there is a secret hatch on every plane that allows me to travel freely throughout the aircraft.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: She took a ride on the carousel.

jokes.text: Give me the hot dog, baby!
jokes.setup: Charlie preparing to bat
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate baseball terminology/sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Standard Charlie weirdness. His bizarre baseball terminology has sexual undertones but isn't particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Give me the hot dog, baby!

jokes.text: That's the Wade Boggs style! ... That's the only ball I have, and I didn't expect you to hit it that far. I really don't feel like going to get it.
jokes.setup: Charlie hitting a dramatic home run
jokes.punchline: Mac's practical concern about ball retrieval ruins the moment
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimatic moment showing Mac's laziness undermining dramatic baseball moment.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's the Wade Boggs style!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's the only ball I have, and I didn't expect you to hit it that far. I really don't feel like going to get it.

jokes.text: So, I'm the commissioner. I say you did it! Game on!
jokes.setup: Mac not wanting to retrieve the ball
jokes.punchline: He uses his made-up authority to avoid work
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's commissioner role, now used for pure convenience.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So, I'm the commissioner. I say you did it! Game on!

jokes.text: You know, that's the only ball I have, and I didn't expect you to hit it that far. I really don't feel like going to get it. So, I'm the commissioner. I say you did it!
jokes.setup: Charlie hitting the ball very far
jokes.punchline: Mac using his fake authority to avoid having to retrieve the ball
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - using his made-up authority for personal convenience. His laziness trumps his dedication to accuracy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know, that's the only ball I have, and I didn't expect you to hit it that far. I really don't feel like going to get it. So, I'm the commissioner. I say you did it!

jokes.text: They got chicken Philly? They got chicken, yeah!
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about food in Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: References his earlier obsession with Wade Boggs eating chicken
jokes.explanation: Minor callback to Charlie's chicken obsession, decent closure but not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: They got chicken Philly? They got chicken, yeah!
